description: We lead on equality policy including women’s equality, race equality, LGBT+ equality, disability equality, socio-economic equality and the overall framework of equality legislation for the UK. OEO works with the Cabinet Office .

details.body:
We lead on equality policy including women’s equality, race equality, LGBT+ equality, disability equality, socio-economic equality and the overall framework of equality legislation for the UK.

OEO works with the Cabinet Office.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Action plans supporting women to succeed at work launched by Minister for Women and Equalities ahead of International Women’s Day 2026.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government launches landmark gender pay gap and menopause action plans to help women thrive at work

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Minister for Women and Equalities Bridget Phillipson has confirmed that Dr Mary-Ann Stephenson will be appointed as the new Chair of the Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC).
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government confirms Dr Mary-Ann Stephenson as the next EHRC chair

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Minister for Equalities Seema Malhotra and Baroness Lawrence hosted the first Race Equality Engagement Group roundtable alongside leaders from ethnic minority communities.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government launches Black History Month alongside ethnic minority leaders to further race equality

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Women’s Business Council (WBC) brings together business leaders who work to boost women’s representation in the workplace.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Women's Business Council refreshed to help break down barriers for opportunity for women at work

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The government has launched new 5-year plans to improve the use of BSL in individual departments to help break down barriers for Deaf and disabled people.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government unveils new era for British Sign Language (BSL) by championing accessible communications

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The views of ethnic minority people are being placed at the heart of government decision making following the establishment of the Race Equality Engagement Group (REEG).
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government announces Race Equality Engagement Group to break down barriers to racial equality

details.description: Our Personal Information Charter (https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/cabinet-office/about/personal-information-charter) explains how we handle your information when you contact us. It also explains how to make a data protection request for your personal information.
details.title: Office for Equality and Opportunity

details.description: Equality Advisory and Support Service (EASS): EASS provides free bespoke advice and in-depth support to people with discrimination problems. You can contact EASS by: - telephone – 0808 800 0082 - text phone – 0808 800 0084 Acas: If your enquiry relates to workplace relations and employment, including workplace discrimination, Acas provides free and impartial advice to employees or applicants. You can contact them: - through the Acas website – https://www.acas.org.uk/contact - on their helpline – 0300 123 1100 Legal advice: You can get independent legal advice by contacting: - Citizens Advice – http://www.citizensadvice.org.uk/ - your local Law Centre – https://www.lawcentres.org.uk/get-help
details.title: Discrimination and employment advice and support

ordered_contacts.title: Discrimination and employment advice and support

image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Bridget Phillipson MP

details.body:
The Minister for Women and Equalities has responsibility for:

  • promoting equality of opportunity for everyone, and reducing negative disparities
  • strategic oversight of the government’s equality policy, for women, ethnicity, disability and LGBT+
  • overall sponsorship of the Social Mobility Commission and Equality and Human Rights Commission
  • overview of the overarching equalities legislative framework

role_appointments.title: Bridget Phillipson MP - Minister for Women and Equalities

details.body:
The Secretary of State is responsible for the work of the Department for Education, including:

  • early years and childcare
  • children’s social care
  • teacher quality, recruitment and retention
  • the school curriculum
  • school improvement
  • academies and free schools
  • further education
  • apprenticeships and skills
  • higher education

role_appointments.title: Bridget Phillipson MP - Secretary of State for Education

ordered_ministers.title: The Rt Hon Bridget Phillipson MP

image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Baroness Smith of Malvern

details.body: Lords spokesperson on equalities.

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Baroness Smith of Malvern - Government spokesperson for Equalities

details.body:
The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • lead policy responsibility for gender equality and women’s rights
  • sponsorship of the Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC) and Social Mobility Commission (SMC)
  • UK equality framework (including the Equality Act)
  • equality data and analysis

The minister also has lead responsibility in the House of Lords on the following policy areas:

  • socioeconomic opportunity
  • race and ethnicity policy
  • Employment Rights Bill (Department for Business and Trade lead)

role.title: Minister of State (Minister for Women and Equalities)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Baroness Smith of Malvern - Minister of State (Minister for Women and Equalities)

details.body:
The minister’s Department for Education (DfE) responsibilities include:

  • governance, intervention and accountability of further education colleges
  • funding for education and training, provision and outcomes for 16- to 19-year-olds
  • further education financial stability and workforce
  • access to higher education, participation and lifelong learning entitlement
  • quality of higher education and the student experience (including the Office for Students)
  • student finance (including the Student Loans Company)
  • technical and vocational qualifications (level 3 and below), including T Levels
  • higher technical education (levels 4 and 5)
  • careers advice
  • support for young people aged 16 to 19 not in employment, education or training (NEET)
  • skills strategy and delivery
  • technical excellence colleges
  • international education

The minister’s Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) responsibilities include:

  • Skills England and Industry Training Boards
  • adult education, including basic skills
  • apprenticeships, including the growth and skills levy
  • skills bootcamps
  • sector skills packages
  • careers advice and support for young people over the age of 19 not in employment, education or training (NEET)
  • Youth Guarantee

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Jacqui Smith - Minister of State

ordered_ministers.title: The Rt Hon Baroness Smith of Malvern

image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Sir Stephen Timms MP

details.body:
• Disability policy and x-Gov responsibility for disabled people 

  • Health and disability reform 

  • Universal Credit and Legacy Benefits Delivery 

  • Contributory Benefits, Personal Independence Payment (PIP), Disability Living Allowance (DLA), and Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) 

  • Carer’s Allowance (CA) 

  • Housing 

  • Access to Work 

  • ALB: Health and Safety Executive 

  • Serious Case Panel 

  • Uprating and Benefit Cap 

  • Oversight of Disability Unit (DU)

role.title: Minister of State (Minister for Social Security and Disability)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Sir Stephen Timms MP - Minister of State

ordered_ministers.title: The Rt Hon Sir Stephen Timms MP

details.body:
The Minister’s responsibilities are:

  • legal migration policy
  • Immigration Rules and visa policy
  • Windrush Compensation Scheme
  • Future Borders and Immigration System
  • HM Passport Office
  • General Register Office
  • Border Force operations
  • safe and legal routes and resettlement

role.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Migration and Citizenship)

role_appointments.title: Seema Malhotra MP - Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Migration and Citizenship)

details.body:
• Indo-Pacific
  • growth
  • global tech and artificial intelligence
  • departmental operations (including Legal)

role_appointments.title: Seema Malhotra MP - Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State

details.body:
• race and ethnicity policy
  • ministerial sponsor of the Race Equality Engagement Group
  • women’s equality policy, including pay gap reporting, equal pay and pay transparency
  • lead minister for the Equality (Race and Disability) Bill

role.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Equalities)

role_appointments.title: Seema Malhotra MP - Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Minister for Equalities)

details.body:
The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • early years education including for children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND)
  • childcare and the home learning environment
  • early years workforce
  • early communication skills and early intervention
  • Best Start Family Hubs and parenting
  • breakfast clubs
  • school food, including free school meals
  • holiday activities and food (HAF) programme
  • school attendance, including register of children who are not in school
  • reception admissions
  • changes to existing schools
  • home-to-school transport
  • mental health support in schools
  • behaviour, preventing bullying and exclusions in schools
  • school uniform
  • young carers
  • independent schools
  • illegal schools
  • faith schools
  • intervention in failing schools and school improvement
  • use of data, digital technology and artificial intelligence (AI) in education
  • use of research, science and evidence at DfE

role.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Early Education)

role_appointments.title: Olivia Bailey MP - Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Early Education)

details.body:
The minister’s responsibilities include:

  • LGBT+ legislation including on conversion practices 
  • LGBT+ policy

role.title: Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Minister for Equalities)

role_appointments.title: Olivia Bailey MP - Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State
